title: Remove buildah run cmd and entrypoint execution
body:
Fixes #607 

Remove the `cmd` and `entrypoint` behaviour when `buildah run` is executed with no arguments, so `buildah run` matches `Dockerfile RUN` behaviour.

Signed-off-by: pixdrift <support@pixeldrift.net>

comments.body:
Is this correct?  We don't want these set for buildah bud either?

I was thinking the simple change would be to modify cmd/buildah/run.go to require a cmd.  But maybe we need both.
@nalind @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL
Any chance you could add your tests?

The block removed looks to fallback to cmd/entrypoint if a command line argument isn't provided. As discussed in #607, this doesn't look to be desired behaviour which is essentially "if no command provided, error".

I have produced updated test outputs and attached to #607

CI test is failure with expected run/entrypoint output.. before updating the tests can I wait for feedback from @nalind and @TomSweeneyRedHat to confirm i'm not crazy?. Please check logs I have put in #607
BTW @pixdrift we live on IRC on freenode on #podman and to a lesser degree #buildah

Not having a fallback would technically be correct (mildly inconvenient for some use cases), but we should definitely continue to heed the `Cmd` and `Entrypoint` values that are passed in via the `RunOptions` struct.
@nalind, can you give an example use case for RunOptions struct being used for execution, or reproducer so I can test/validate?
@nalind You are saying we should leave this code as is, and fix this at the cmd/buildah/run.go?
It's something we pass in from imagebuilder (which is how 'bud' integrates with it), but now that you mention it, I'm not sure that it'd ever pass us an empty `command` slice.

I'm a bit confused by the expected behaviour of setting CMD and ENTRY_POINT in different places and how it effects run.  We should really document/blog this somewhere.  Sounds like a great tutorial subject.

@nalind This all started based off of a naked RUN line in a docker file

See #607
Sounds like those fields don't need to be in `RunOptions`, then.
Yes I don't think they should be there.  They are more for the image, but not for individual RUN commands in the Dockerfile.

Just want to verify, the past few weeks I've been leaning on this article: http://www.johnzaccone.io/entrypoint-vs-cmd-back-to-basics/ to figure out entrypoint/cmd behaviour.  In summary when you do buildah/docker run:

1)    if neither is set in the image, then error.
2)    If one and only one is set, then they have the same effect and are run.
3)    There are shell and exec versions for both 
       a) shell form: "ping www.google.com"
       b) exec form: ["ping", "www.google.com"]
       c) exec form recommended
4)    Shell and Exec together get interesting:
        a) shell form CMD is ignored
        b) exec form, CMD is appended to ENTRYPOINT
5)    Either or both CMD and ENTRYPOINT can be over-ridden by command line flags.

Is this the above the expect behaviour for Buildah?  I'm not sure we're following this 100% at this point.  Although with point 1, I don't think we error, but currently open run a shell.

No buildah run is different, what you describe above is fine for podman run and docker run. But I don't think buildah run or RUN flag in dockerfile should do anything with entrypoint or command. They only execute the command given to them. If no command is given then it should fail.  This is why we should insist on a command in cmd/buildah/run.go.

@TomSweeneyRedHat, as mentioned in the #607 issue the expected behaviour as explained by @rhatdan is:
```
buildah run = Dockerfile RUN
podman run = docker run
```

Which results in `buildah run` behaviour not being modified/affected by a configured `cmd` or `entrypoint`. The idea is the `buildah run` is the shell equivalent of `RUN` in a dockerfile.

Agree this should be documented. As I mentioned on IRC, the following Fedora blog post had an expectation that `buildah run` would execute `entrypoint` (last command in tutorial).
https://fedoramagazine.org/daemon-less-container-management-buildah/#comment-476544
CI tests have been updated, and additional test cases included. Looking at changing the command line behaviour now.. is it preferred that this change be rolled into this PR? or a separate PR?
Either is fine.  If this passes I will merge.

@rhatdan, `run` has been updated to require a command, PTAL. Tests were also updated to validate new error message.

I have added tests for both explicit end of argument option (`--`) and without.

Following tests were added
```
ok 33 bud with ENTRYPOINT and RUN
ok 34 bud with ENTRYPOINT and empty RUN
ok 35 bud with CMD and RUN
ok 36 bud with CMD and empty RUN
```

@rhatdan, CI failure is unrelated. Please re-run tests when you get a chance.

LGTM
@rh-atomic-bot r+

:pushpin: Commit aaafb35 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan aaafb3509e2fa785bed6c580c1a3b61ad614921a -->
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.

I've not tested, but I'm pretty sure this change will break the functionality listed in our tutorial, our baseline tests, a few blogs, and possibly in @ipbabble 's demos.  I don't think the following from the tutorial will work with this change....

```
# buildah copy $newcontainer ./runecho.sh /usr/bin
# buildah config --cmd /usr/bin/runecho.sh $newcontainer

Now run the container:

# buildah run $newcontainer
```

What’s the motivation for this?

Must we mimic Dockerfile behavior? Why?

I don’t think changing tutorials et al would be too difficult except that
we then need to introduce Podman in some of these demos.

I’ve kinda been doing that in recent demos. But I want to make sure there
is a solid case for this, rather than me-too type compliance with
dockerfile. Also does this mean we have to move functionality somewhere
else? If Buildah doesn’t do it then does it have to me to Podman?

William

On Mon, May 7, 2018 at 7:36 AM Tom Sweeney <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> I've not tested, but I'm pretty sure this change will break the
> functionality listed in our tutorial, our baseline tests, a few blogs, and
> possibly in @ipbabble <https://github.com/ipbabble> 's demos. I don't
> think the following from the tutorial will work with this change....
>
> # buildah copy $newcontainer ./runecho.sh /usr/bin
> # buildah config --cmd /usr/bin/runecho.sh $newcontainer
>
> Now run the container:
>
> # buildah run $newcontainer
>

> .

-- 
Sent from Gmail Mobile on iPhone

Basically we want 
buildah run == Dockerfile RUN
podman run == docker run
Lets fix the tutorials.

Ok. I see from a post you made a few days ago that has an explanation. 

I'm still not sure why buildah run must == Dockerfile RUN. I have found it handy that it did more. Having said that, now that podman is super functional it really does help separate the difference. 

I'll update the tutorials. I assume I need to fix/add updates to the ProjectAtomic blogs. Maybe fix and then put some footnotes about why.
